issues.title: Undocumented limitation when using Intelligent Terminal with Copilot on Windows 11
issues.next_action_reason: Blocked community reporter supplied logs revealing root cause (no CLI on PATH); explain + acknowledge doc gap.
issues.suggested_reply: @Sybelius thank you for the logs - they show the key detail: `_IsAgentInstalled: copilot/claude/codex/gemini NOT found on PATH`. Intelligent Terminal doesn't ship its own model; it drives an agent CLI you install. With no CLI on PATH it falls back to the built-in Copilot path, which is what surfaces the "requires enterprise/org policy" message. Please install and sign into an agent CLI (e.g. GitHub Copilot CLI or Claude Code) so it's on PATH, then relaunch. We'll also improve the docs/error to make this explicit - tracking that here.

issues.title: ZoomIt activates on AltGr+key instead of Ctrl+key
issues.next_action_reason: Community has supplied version, repro, and root cause plus images; team owes acknowledgement + routing. Confirm root cause, offer remap workaround, and request exact layout/keys to reproduce.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the detailed reports and screenshots. To confirm the root cause: on AZERTY/QWERTZ layouts Windows generates AltGr as Ctrl+Alt, so characters like `(`, `[`, `]`, `\` typed via AltGr collide with ZoomIt's Ctrl-based shortcuts (e.g. `Ctrl+(` = Record window), which is why ZoomIt intercepts them. issues.title: WSL 2 mirrored networking breaks NFS mounts ("Connection timed out")
issues.next_action_reason: Long-standing high-reach networking bug with community-verified root cause + workaround that the team has never acknowledged; ack the workaround, add labels, and prioritize a real fix.
issues.suggested_reply:
Thanks everyone for the detailed investigation here. Summarizing the community findings for the team and other users:

- Root cause appears to be that in mirrored networking mode, NFS mounts using privileged (reserved, <1024) source ports fail to complete the TCP handshake (SYN+ACK not delivered to the WSL socket).
- Confirmed workaround: mount with the `noresvport` option, e.g. `sudo mount -t nfs -o noresvport <HOST>:/share /mnt/share` (also works via `/etc/auto.net` / autofs and docker-compose).

We'll add `workaround-available` and keep this tracked for a proper fix. Could someone still hitting this on the latest 2.7.x confirm whether `noresvport` resolves it for them so we can prioritize?

issues.title: 🚨 WSLg GPU Acceleration Fails on Intel UHD 620 (WDDM 3.1) — dxgkrnl Ioctl Errors, Falls Back to llvmpipe
issues.next_action_reason: Long-unanswered community GPU thread with a confirmed workaround; acknowledge, share the GALLIUM_DRIVER=d3d12 workaround, and add the GPU label.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the detailed report, @ian1roberts, and to everyone who added their hardware details. On older Intel iGPUs (UHD/HD 6xx, WDDM before full GPU-PV support) the D3D12 Gallium driver often can't initialize, so WSLg falls back to llvmpipe (software). As @javiersevillanoizq-lab and others confirmed, forcing the D3D12 Gallium driver — `echo 'export GALLIUM_DRIVER=d3d12' >> ~/.bashrc` — restores hardware acceleration on these GPUs; please also make sure your Intel graphics driver is fully up to date. We'll label this as a GPU issue so it's tracked against the driver-init path.
